current_run.description: Given an N-bit input x and a secret N-bit integer s, the oracle computes a boolean function f(x) which is the parity of a sum of linear and cyclic quadratic terms. Specifically, f(x) = ( sum_{i=0}^{N-1} (s_i * x_i) + sum_{i=0}^{N-1} (s_i * x_i * x_{i+1}) ) % 2, where s_i and x_i are the i-th bits of s and x respectively, and indices are modulo N (i.e., x_N = x_0). The goal is to discover the secret 's' using a quantum algorithm.
